name: Verb + -ing
summary: **After these verbs, the next verb ends in -ing** (enjoy **reading**, avoid **making**). **Never** use **to + verb** in this lesson.
contrast_note: **Different lesson:** **Verb + to + verb** (want **to go**). **Same verb, two meanings?** **Tricky verbs: different meaning**. **Both forms OK, same idea?** **Both forms: same meaning**.

name: Verb + to + verb
summary: **After these verbs, use to + base verb** (want **to go**, decide **to stay**). **Not** **-ing** in this lesson.
contrast_note: **Different lesson:** **Verb + -ing** (enjoy **reading**). **Same verb, two meanings?** **Tricky verbs: different meaning**. **Both forms OK, same idea?** **Both forms: same meaning**.

name: Tricky verbs: different meaning
structure: Same verb: **-ing** or **to + verb** — different meanings
summary: Some verbs allow **-ing** **or** **to + verb**. **Here the meaning changes** — so read the **sentence**, not only the grammar.
contrast_note: **Not** **Both forms: same meaning**: there the two forms feel **almost the same**. **Not** lessons 1–2: those verbs take **only** **-ing** or **only** **to + verb**.

name: Both forms: same meaning
summary: **You can often use either form.** The meaning stays **about the same**. No need to stress — this is **easier** than **Tricky verbs**.
contrast_note: **Tricky verbs** = same verb, **different ideas**. **Lessons 1–2** = **only** **-ing** or **only** **to + verb**.

name: Preposition + -ing
summary: Look at the word **before** the verb (**in**, **at**, **after**, **interested in**…). **After that, use -ing** — not **to + verb**.
contrast_note: **Verb + -ing** (lesson 1) = the **main verb** decides (**enjoy playing**). **Here** = the **preposition** decides (**interested in learning**). Different place, same **-ing** sound — **different rule**.
